rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture and ownership when adding an annex block; fuse content only into a valid owner scope.
Use the input AADL model as the authority for component kinds, names, features, implementations, and existing structure before modifying a target.
Keep type-scope contract content separate from implementation-scope content; do not move statements across scopes during fusion.
If adding an AGREE annex, maintain valid annex block structure without altering unrelated declarations or connections.
generic_rules: Perform minimal edits to the intended owner and preserve all unrelated model text and hierarchy.
Do not introduce references that depend on symbols outside the chosen owner's valid scope.
Respect existing component type versus implementation boundaries when merging generated content.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-level content belongs only in implementation scope; feature-level contract content belongs in type scope.
anti_patterns: Do not fuse content into the wrong owner or duplicate annex blocks unnecessarily.
Do not rewrite architecture, subcomponents, connections, or properties when the task is only annex insertion.
Do not rely on AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were AGREE variables during fusion.
irrelevant_topics: Validator-specific type-repair tactics unless diagnostics are provided.
Requirement-specific numeric bounds or clause selection decisions for the current case.
